input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

### Xcode (for iOS)
- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode **and** the Command Line Tools:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  ```
- After install, open Xcode once to accept the license and install additional components.
- Verify: `xcodebuild -version`

### JDK (for Android)
- .NET MAUI's Android workload bundles a JDK automatically (Microsoft OpenJDK). You typically **don't** need to install one separately. If you do, use **Microsoft OpenJDK 17**:
  - [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)

---

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the **latest .NET SDK** (currently .NET 9).
- Use the macOS installer (`.pkg`).
- Verify:
  ```
  dotnet --version
  dotnet --list-sdks
  ```

### Discover the right version
```
dotnet workload search maui
```
This shows available MAUI workload versions compatible with your SDK.

---

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in **all** sub-workloads: `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, `maui-maccatalyst`, plus the Android SDK, build tools, and emulator images.

Verify:
```
dotnet workload list
```
You should see `maui` (or the individual sub-workloads) listed.

---

## 4. Android SDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload installs the Android SDK automatically under:
```
~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx/
```

To manage it manually or add emulator images, you can use:
```
dotnet android sdk list
dotnet android sdk install "system-images;android-35;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
dotnet android avd create --name Pixel_API_35 --package "system-images;android-35;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

Or install **Android Studio** ([developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)) for a GUI-based SDK/AVD manager. This is optional but helpful.

---

## 5. iOS Simulator

Comes with Xcode. Verify available simulators:
```
xcrun simctl list devices
```

---

## 6. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been **retired**. VS Code with the C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension is now the recommended free option on macOS.

---

## 7. Create & Verify a Test Project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui
```

**Build check:**
```bash
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

**Run on iOS Simulator:**
```bash
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios
```

**Run on Android Emulator:**
```bash
# Start emulator first
dotnet android avd start --name Pixel_API_35 &
# Then run
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

---

## 8. Troubleshooting Checklist

| Issue | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| `xcode-select: error` | Run `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app` |
| Workload restore fails | Run `sudo dotnet workload repair` |
| Android SDK not found | Set `ANDROID_HOME=~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx/` |
| Outdated workloads | Run `sudo dotnet workload update` |
| Check all dependencies | Run `dotnet maui check` (via the `maui-check` tool or built-in) |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → create a project → build & run. The workload handles Android SDK/JDK automatically.
